reason_codes: main_mlp.py: selected for non-linear function approximation and deep-model training; serves as the primary metric observer (training loss, validation accuracy, logging hooks). Relevant claims: claim_11, claim_12, claim_13, claim_14, claim_15, claim_16, claim_17.

main_logistic_regression.py: selected to provide a probabilistic/classification baseline with evaluation metrics (accuracy, AUC) for comparison to the MLP observer. Relevant claims: claim_18, claim_19, claim_20, claim_21.

main_linear_regression.py: selected to provide a simple continuous-output baseline (MSE, R^2) and to support claims about regression workflows and metric reporting. Relevant claims: claim_23, claim_24, claim_25.

main_gru_svm.py: selected to cover sequence-modeling and hybrid approaches (GRU + SVM) for claims related to sequential data handling and combined architectures. Relevant claims: claim_26, claim_28, claim_29.

main_nearest_neighbor.py: selected to include an instance-based, interpretable baseline (k-NN) useful for small-data regimes and for robustness/interpretability claims. Relevant claims: claim_30, claim_33, claim_34, claim_35, claim_36, claim_37, claim_38, claim_39, claim_40.
